# QC-05: Code Quality Standards

**Track:** Quality Controls (`QC`) · **Priority:** SHOULD (recommended) · **Status:** draft

Enforce automated code quality standards continuously during implementation: linting, cyclomatic complexity thresholds, coding standards compliance, and maintainability metrics. Blocks commits that fall below defined quality thresholds.

## Required In Stages

| Stage | Name | Applicability |
|-------|------|---------------|
| 3 | Coding & Implementation | Mandatory |

## Delegation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Pattern** | Fully automated |
| **Agent does** | Run quality checks on every commit; report violations; block commits that exceed thresholds. |
| **Human does** | Configure thresholds; review and triage recurring violations; update standards as needed. |

## Outputs

| Artifact | Type | Description | Retention |
|----------|------|-------------|-----------|
| Quality gate report | evidence | Per-commit quality scan results including metrics and any threshold violations. | 7 years (DORA) |


## Automation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Frequency** | per_change |
| **SLA** | — |
| **Tooling** | Linter (language-specific), Complexity analyser, Standards checker |

## Failure Behaviour

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **On failure** | block |
| **Escalate to** | Tech Lead |

Commits failing quality thresholds are blocked. Developer must resolve violations before re-committing.

## Dependencies

None

## Regulatory Mapping

| Regulation | Article | Obligation |
|------------|---------|------------|
| DORA | Art. 8(4) | ICT systems must be built to minimise ICT risk through quality controls |
| EU AI Act | Art. 15(1) | AI systems must be designed for appropriate levels of accuracy and reliability |


## Ownership

**Owner team:** Engineering · **Owner role:** Tech Lead
